threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still call it 'the Tube' instead of just 'the Underground' like a true Hackneyer. And good luck finding a bus that runs on time.
Last night on the 59, an old bloke argued with a raccoon over the last donut in a takeaway bag. The raccoon was winning.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The day I saw three kids playing in a puddle while their grandma bargained over fruits at the market, I knew I was home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a currywurst stand with vegan options open at 3 AM. It's a lifesaver after a night out!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s local when they're unfazed by the dog on the tracks at Civic Center but lose their mind over a latte being $5.75.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a Muni bus today, and a guy serenading us with a ukulele. Only in the Tenderloin would a delay sound like a jam session.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is just about the parks or squares. What they miss is the late-night life in the side streets—real conversations happen over cheap beer.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: They still say 'gràcies' with a heavy accent at the markets, and struggle with the speed of the 'hola' on the street.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think the bus drivers will help you with directions. Just keep an eye out; they're not your tour guides, mate.
Caught the 59 last night; didn’t expect a guy to serenade the whole bus with 90s R&B. Some joined in. Only in Brixton, right?

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I realized I was home? When I heard a street performer covering 90s Eurodance while kids joyfully danced in the rain at Hermannplatz.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain do you find a bear-shaped fountain surrounded by graffiti murals, where kids splash and skaters chill. It's a quirky little gem.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: A true local knows to avoid the Civic Center BART during rush hour—too many 'hands up, don’t touch' moments with the overheated brakes.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 19 earlier and a guy was serenading the bus with a ukulele. Only in SF would a lullaby clash with a loud horn outside.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think every street in Gràcia is a photo op, but the real charm lies in the small, hidden plazas where locals gather over cheap beers.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a newcomer by the way they still ask for a 'tapa' and expect a plate instead of a tiny portion. We all did it once.
